mainText:
**Orbán Loses Hungarian Election**
Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán conceded defeat to Péter Magyar after Hungarian voters on Sunday overwhelmingly voted his government out of office, marking the end of Orbán's 16-year leadership. Magyar’s pro-European, right-of-center Tisza party is expected to win 138 of the 199 seats in Hungary’s legislature, achieving a supermajority with more than double the number of seats won by Orbán’s nationalist populist Fidesz party. Election officials estimated a record-setting turnout of 79 percent. With this majority, Magyar’s party could implement constitutional amendments and reforms.

**Reactions**
European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen stated, "Hungary has chosen Europe. Europe has always chosen Hungary," emphasizing the strengthening of the Union. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky congratulated Magyar, highlighting the importance of constructive approaches prevailing in such contexts. Donald Trump had publicly endorsed Orbán's reelection, supported by Vice President J.D. Vance, who campaigned in Hungary last week.

**Trump Blockades Hormuz**
Former President Donald Trump ordered the Navy to blockade the Strait of Hormuz after U.S.-Iran negotiations, mediated by Pakistan, collapsed. Speaking from Islamabad, Vice President Vance confirmed that after 21 hours of discussions, Iran did not agree to U.S. terms, including a commitment to refrain from developing nuclear weapons. Axios reported that Iran refused to surrender its enriched uranium stockpiles and control over the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. Central Command announced the blockade would be enforced impartially against vessels of all nations traveling to or from Iranian ports, starting at 10 a.m. ET on Monday. However, it would not impede traffic between other Gulf nations. British Prime Minister Keir Starmer stated Britain would not support the blockade.

**U.S. Inflation Increases**
The consumer price index increased by 3.3 percent year-over-year in March, its highest annual figure since May 2024, according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics. This marks a rise of 0.9 percentage points from February and aligns with analysts' forecasts. Key contributors to this increase included energy prices, which surged by 10.9 percent from February to March, with gas prices alone rising 21.2 percent during this timeframe. The fuel oil index saw its largest monthly leap since February 2000, increasing by 30.7 percent. Core CPI, excluding volatile energy and food prices, rose by 2.6 percent year-over-year.

**Swalwell Suspends Governor Campaign After Sexual Assault Allegations**
Rep. Eric Swalwell of California suspended his gubernatorial campaign following allegations of sexual misconduct from four women. A former staff member accused Swalwell of raping her while heavily intoxicated in a New York City hotel in April 2024 and of a sexual assault in 2019. Swalwell has denied these accusations, describing them as “flat false.” Three additional women reported that he sent them unsolicited explicit photos and engaged in unwanted kissing. The Manhattan District Attorney’s Office is investigating the allegations. Following the reports, Swalwell's campaign chair resigned and all 21 of his Democratic endorsers withdrew their support.

**Three Injured in Grand Central Machete Attack**
A 44-year-old man armed with a machete injured three individuals—including an 84-year-old man—at Grand Central Station in New York City. The attacker was shot dead by police following the incident. Male victims sustained lacerations, and the female victim suffered a cut on her shoulder, but none were reported to have life-threatening injuries. Police Commissioner Jessica Tisch stated that officers attempted to de-escalate the situation before resorting to lethal force. The attacker was reportedly exhibiting erratic behavior and claiming to be “Lucifer.” NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani expressed gratitude to the police for their swift action to prevent further violence.
